SO-1.name: Data Residency
SO-1.description: Ensure data stays within approved geographic regions. Enforces allowed locations for resources and resource groups.

SO-2.name: Customer Lockbox
SO-2.description: Require customer approval before Microsoft support can access customer data. No Azure Policy available — procedural control only.

SO-3.name: Customer-Managed Keys
SO-3.description: Encrypt data at rest using customer-managed keys (CMK) instead of Microsoft-managed keys.

SO-4.name: Confidential Computing
SO-4.description: Restrict deployments to confidential computing-capable resource types and VM SKUs for encryption in-use.

SO-5.name: Trusted Launch
SO-5.description: Require Trusted Launch for virtual machines to protect against boot-level attacks.

built_in_initiatives.description: Built-in Azure Policy initiative enforcing SO-1 (Data Residency) and SO-5 (Trusted Launch) for all sovereign workloads.
Built-in Azure Policy initiative enforcing SO-1 (Data Residency), SO-3 (CMK), and SO-4 (Confidential Computing).

policy_definitions.name: allowed-locations
policy_definitions.display_name: Allowed locations
policy_definitions.description: This policy enables you to restrict the locations your organization can specify when deploying resources.

policy_definitions.name: allowed-locations-for-resource-groups
policy_definitions.display_name: Allowed locations for resource groups
policy_definitions.description: This policy enables you to restrict the locations your organization can create resource groups in.

policy_definitions.name: cmk-recovery-services
policy_definitions.display_name: Azure Recovery Services vaults should use customer-managed keys for encrypting backup data
policy_definitions.description: Use customer-managed keys to manage the encryption at rest of your backup data.

policy_definitions.name: cmk-container-instances
policy_definitions.display_name: Azure container instance container group should use customer-managed key for encryption
policy_definitions.description: Secure your containers with greater flexibility using customer-managed keys.

policy_definitions.name: cmk-aks
policy_definitions.display_name: Azure Kubernetes Service clusters should use customer-managed keys for disk encryption
policy_definitions.description: Encrypt at rest OS and data disks of AKS cluster nodes with customer-managed keys.

policy_definitions.name: cmk-managed-disks
policy_definitions.display_name: Managed disks should be double encrypted with both platform-managed and customer-managed keys
policy_definitions.description: Customers can select double encryption with both platform- and customer-managed keys.

policy_definitions.name: cmk-mysql
policy_definitions.display_name: MySQL servers should use customer-managed keys to encrypt data at rest
policy_definitions.description: Use customer-managed keys to manage the encryption at rest of your MySQL servers.

policy_definitions.name: cmk-os-data-disks
policy_definitions.display_name: OS and data disks should be encrypted with a customer-managed key
policy_definitions.description: Use customer-managed keys to manage the encryption at rest of the contents of your managed disks.

policy_definitions.name: cmk-postgresql
policy_definitions.display_name: PostgreSQL servers should use customer-managed keys to encrypt data at rest
policy_definitions.description: Use customer-managed keys to manage the encryption at rest of your PostgreSQL servers.

policy_definitions.name: cmk-queue-storage
policy_definitions.display_name: Queue Storage should use customer-managed key for encryption
policy_definitions.description: Secure your queue storage with greater flexibility using customer-managed keys.

policy_definitions.name: cmk-sql-mi
policy_definitions.display_name: SQL managed instances should use customer-managed keys to encrypt data at rest
policy_definitions.description: Use customer-managed keys for TDE on your SQL managed instances.

policy_definitions.name: cmk-sql-server
policy_definitions.display_name: SQL servers should use customer-managed keys to encrypt data at rest
policy_definitions.description: Use customer-managed keys for TDE on your SQL servers.

policy_definitions.name: cmk-storage-scope
policy_definitions.display_name: Storage account encryption scopes should use customer-managed keys to encrypt data at rest
policy_definitions.description: Use customer-managed keys for encryption scopes on storage accounts.

policy_definitions.name: cmk-storage-account
policy_definitions.display_name: Storage accounts should use customer-managed key for encryption
policy_definitions.description: Secure your blob and file storage with customer-managed keys.

policy_definitions.name: cmk-table-storage
policy_definitions.display_name: Table Storage should use customer-managed key for encryption
policy_definitions.description: Secure your table storage with greater flexibility using customer-managed keys.

policy_definitions.name: cmk-hpc-cache
policy_definitions.display_name: HPC Cache accounts should use customer-managed key for encryption
policy_definitions.description: Manage encryption at rest of Azure HPC Cache with customer-managed keys.

policy_definitions.name: cmk-cosmos-db
policy_definitions.display_name: Azure Cosmos DB accounts should use customer-managed keys to encrypt data at rest
policy_definitions.description: Use customer-managed keys to manage the encryption at rest of your Azure Cosmos DB.

policy_definitions.name: allowed-resource-types-cc
policy_definitions.display_name: Allowed resource types
policy_definitions.description: Restrict resource types to only those supporting confidential computing.

policy_definitions.name: allowed-vm-skus-cc
policy_definitions.display_name: Allowed virtual machine size SKUs
policy_definitions.description: Restrict VM SKU sizes to only confidential computing-capable series.

policy_definitions.name: disk-os-trustedlaunch
policy_definitions.display_name: Disks and OS image should support TrustedLaunch
policy_definitions.description: Ensure managed disks and OS images support Trusted Launch.

policy_definitions.name: vm-trustedlaunch
policy_definitions.display_name: Virtual Machine should have TrustedLaunch enabled
policy_definitions.description: Enable TrustedLaunch on virtual machine to protect from boot-level attacks.

policy_set_definitions.name: Sovereignty-Baseline-Global
policy_set_definitions.description: Enforces SO-1 (Data Residency) and SO-5 (Trusted Launch) across all sovereign workloads.

policy_set_definitions.name: Sovereignty-Baseline-Confidential
policy_set_definitions.description: Enforces SO-1, SO-3 (CMK), and SO-4 (Confidential Computing) for confidential workloads.

policy_assignments.name: Enforce-Sovereign-Global
policy_assignments.description: Assigns the Sovereignty Baseline Global initiative at sovereignty root.

policy_assignments.name: Enforce-Sovereign-Conf
policy_assignments.description: Assigns the Sovereignty Baseline Confidential initiative at confidential MGs.

archetypes.name: Sovereign Root
archetypes.description: Top-level management group. Inherits ALZ root and adds sovereignty global policies.

archetypes.name: Confidential Corp
archetypes.description: Connected workloads requiring confidential computing protections.

archetypes.name: Confidential Online
archetypes.description: Internet-facing workloads requiring confidential computing protections.

archetypes.name: Public
archetypes.description: Standard non-sovereign workloads with basic guardrails.
